assertions.reason: `cargo test --test core_errors` passed `api_status_errors_preserve_taxonomy_and_server_payload`, and the test source verifies 400/401/403/404/409/422/429/500 map to distinct API error kinds while preserving status, headers, request ID, and parsed API error payload fields.

`cargo test --test core_errors` passed `transport_failures_stay_distinct_from_api_status_failures`, which asserts `ErrorKind::Transport`, preserves an underlying source error, and reports no status code or API payload on connection failure.

`cargo test --test core_response_meta` passed both `request_id_is_exposed_on_success_and_error` and `missing_request_id_header_returns_none_without_failing`, proving success metadata returns `Some(x-request-id)` when present and `None` when absent.

`cargo test --test core_errors` passed `api_status_errors_preserve_taxonomy_and_server_payload`, whose status matrix includes representative 429 and 500 responses and asserts the surfaced error preserves the injected `x-request-id`.

`cargo test --test core_response_meta` passed `raw_response_access_preserves_status_headers_and_parsed_data`, showing the same successful response exposes status/header metadata and the parsed typed body without divergence.

`cargo test --test core_errors` passed `malformed_success_responses_raise_parse_errors`, which covers both malformed JSON and schema-mismatched 2xx bodies and asserts `ErrorKind::Parse` rather than API-status success.

commands.observation: The `core_errors` test binary finished successfully with 5/5 tests passing, including `api_status_errors_preserve_taxonomy_and_server_payload`, `transport_failures_stay_distinct_from_api_status_failures`, and `malformed_success_responses_raise_parse_errors`.

The `core_response_meta` test binary finished successfully with 3/3 tests passing, including `request_id_is_exposed_on_success_and_error`, `raw_response_access_preserves_status_headers_and_parsed_data`, and `missing_request_id_header_returns_none_without_failing`.

summary: All 6 assigned core error/metadata assertions were confirmed by passing mocked cargo tests and their assertion-specific test coverage.
